action.thought_summary: Starting interview. Need to understand the current website's purpose, users, and key workflows to identify implicit requirements around interaction, content, and style.
action.question: What is the primary purpose of the current website, and who are its main user groups (e.g., customers, employees, general public)?

action.thought_summary: User has a company brochure site with pages like homepage, about us, products, news. They want search and easy browsing. Need to probe interaction details, specifically about the search functionality they mentioned since it's a key feature.
action.question: You mentioned users should be able to search for information — what types of content should the search cover (e.g., product listings only, news articles, all page content), and should results display in any particular way?

action.thought_summary: Turn 3 - should probe interaction behavior. I know about search and pages, but haven't explored navigation patterns, how users move between pages, or other interaction behaviors like menus, filters, or mobile interaction.
action.question: How should users navigate between pages — for example, should there be a main navigation menu at the top, a sidebar, breadcrumbs, or footer links? And should the site work well on mobile devices with touch-friendly navigation?

action.thought_summary: Moving to content/data category. User mentioned products and news pages but hasn't specified what information these contain. Probing product page data fields to uncover content structure requirements.
action.question: On the products page, what specific information should each product display — for example, product name, description, images, price, specifications, or download links? And should products be organized into categories that users can filter by?

action.thought_summary: User has been vague on specifics. Currently in content/data coverage phase (turn 5). Need to probe news page content details and then move to style/UI. Will ask about news article structure and organization since products were unclear.
action.question: For the news page, should each news article display a publication date, author name, and summary excerpt? And should news articles be organized chronologically, or grouped by topics/tags that users can filter by?

action.thought_summary: The user is uncertain about many specific details. I've covered interaction (search, navigation, responsive), content (pages, media types), but haven't probed contact/communication features which are typical for brochure sites. I should also explore style/UI preferences soon per the coverage schedule. Let me ask about contact functionality first, as it's a common implicit need for company brochure sites.
action.question: Should the website include a contact page or form where visitors can send inquiries to the company — and if so, what information should visitors be required to provide (e.g., name, email, message subject)?

action.thought_summary: User has been vague on specifics about content and navigation. Moving to style/UI category per coverage schedule. Will ask about visual/branding preferences since they're replacing an existing site.
action.question: Since you're replacing the existing website, are there specific visual or branding elements that must be carried over — such as the company logo, brand colors, or a particular font style? Or are you open to a completely new visual design?

action.thought_summary: The user has provided some specifics on visual style (cotton background, maroon components) and general content types, but hasn't addressed content management. For a brochure site replacement, an implicit requirement is often how non-technical staff will update content after launch. This is a practical content/workflow question.
action.question: After the new website launches, will non-technical staff need to update content themselves (like adding new news articles or products), or will all changes go through a developer? In other words, do you need an admin panel or content management system?

action.thought_summary: The user has been uncertain about many details. I should probe about the existing site's functionality that must be retained, as this was mentioned in the initial requirement but not explored. Also need to cover accessibility and language needs which haven't been asked about yet.
action.question: You mentioned retaining the original functionality — does the current website have any interactive features beyond browsing pages and searching, such as a contact form, downloadable brochures, a newsletter signup, or links to social media profiles that must be carried over?

action.thought_summary: The user has confirmed basic pages, content types, responsive design, search, and visual style (cotton background, maroon components). They've been unsure about many specifics. I should probe accessibility requirements, which is an important implicit need for modern websites, before considering finishing.
action.question: Should the website meet any accessibility standards — for example, ensuring that visually impaired users can navigate using screen readers, that text has sufficient contrast against the cotton background, or that all images have descriptive alt text?

action.thought_summary: The user has been consistently unsure about many specifics. I've covered interaction, content, and style categories. Let me probe a concrete scenario that might reveal implicit requirements - the homepage is a key page that hasn't been detailed yet, and what appears there often reveals priorities and hidden needs.
action.question: When a visitor first lands on the homepage, what should they see immediately — for example, should there be a large banner or hero image, a summary of the company's key message, featured products, or the latest news headlines? What is the most important thing you want first-time visitors to notice?

action.thought_summary: The user has been consistently unsure about many specific details. I need to try a different approach with more concrete, scenario-based questions. Since this is a company brochure site, multilingual support could be an implicit requirement that hasn't been discussed yet. Let me probe this area with a specific question.
action.question: Should the website support multiple languages — for example, if the company serves international clients, would visitors need to switch between English and other languages like Spanish or Chinese?

action.thought_summary: The user has consistently responded with uncertainty to most specific questions, confirming only: brochure site with homepage/about/products/news pages, text/images/videos content, search across all content, responsive design, cotton background with maroon components. I've covered interaction, content, and style categories. Let me try one more concrete probe about a common brochure site element—footer content/legal pages—before considering finishing, as this might reveal implicit requirements about privacy policy, terms, or social media links.
action.question: Should the website include a footer section on every page with standard elements like copyright notice, privacy policy link, and terms of use link — or are there specific legal or compliance pages that the company requires?

action.thought_summary: The user has consistently responded with uncertainty to detailed questions across interaction, content, and style categories. Key confirmed requirements: brochure site with homepage/about/products/news pages, text/images/videos content, search across all content, responsive design, cotton background with maroon components. After 13 turns with limited new information, I should try one more targeted question about a practical aspect of the site replacement, then consider finishing.
action.question: Since you're replacing an existing website, does the current site have any third-party integrations that must continue working — such as Google Analytics for tracking visitor statistics, a live chat widget, or embedded social media feeds?

action.thought_summary: After 14 turns, the user has consistently responded with uncertainty to most specific probes. The confirmed requirements are: company brochure site, specific pages (homepage, about us, products, news, others), content types (text, images, videos), search across all content, responsive design with touch-friendly navigation, cotton background with maroon components, and retaining original content/functionality. Further questioning is unlikely to yield new information.
action.finish_summary: Elicited requirements for the website replacement project: 1) Purpose: Company brochure site replacing an existing website, retaining original content and functionality. 2) Pages: Homepage, About Us, Products, News, and other pages. 3) Content types: Text, images, and videos across pages. 4) Search: Should cover all pages and all content types (text, images, videos). 5) Responsive design: Must adapt to different devices and screen sizes with touch-friendly mobile navigation. 6) Visual style: Cotton background with maroon components on all pages. 7) The user was uncertain about: specific navigation structure, product/news page details, contact form needs, CMS requirements, accessibility standards, homepage layout, multi-language support, footer/legal pages, third-party integrations, and interactive features beyond browsing and searching.
